events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need produce JSON answer. Need assess block/brick laying commercial project, observed field condition: bond pattern broken. Work rough-complete. Reference TMS 402/602. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance.

We need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Bond pattern broken is a defect in masonry. In running bond, units must overlap by at least 1/4 length? TMS 602 Specification for Masonry Structures: For concrete masonry, head joints must be in running bond with units overlapping by not less than 2 inches? Let's recall. TMS 602 (formerly ACI 530.1/ASCE 6) Article 3.3? For unit masonry, running bond: masonry laid such that head joints in successive courses are horizontally offset at least 2 inches (51 mm) or one-quarter unit length, whichever is greater? Actually for brick, running bond requires head joints offset by at least one-quarter of the unit length, or 2 in. For concrete masonry, standard is overlap at least 2 inches or 1/4 length. Broken bond pattern means not maintaining running bond; head joints align vertically or insufficient overlap. This is non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk maybe medium or high? Structural/performance: reduces wall strength, crack resistance, ties. Commercial project with inspections. Could be high if loadbearing/shear. But generic bond pattern broken: medium to high. Need choose. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 80%. Value maybe offset measurement? We need estimate measurable condition. We can set value to e.g. 0 (head joints aligned, no overlap) or insufficient overlap. But schema says value numeric reading or computed quantity. We can set value to 0 inches overlap or maybe 1 inch. Let's set value 0 representing broken bond / no overlap. Confidence 0.9. Sound etc irrelevant set defaults. Findings: head joints aligned vertically, bond pattern not maintained, violates running bond requirements. Actions: remove and rebuild affected courses in running bond, verify overlap per TMS 602, re-inspect. Rationale: TMS 602 requires masonry in running bond with head joints offset minimum 2 in. or 1/4 unit length; broken bond reduces in-plane strength and load distribution and is visible defect. References: TMS 602 Section 3.3? Need be accurate-ish. Let's recall TMS 602-16 Article 3.3 B? Actually TMS 602-16: Part 3 Execution. 3.3 Unit Masonry: 3.3 A? Let's search memory. The Masonry Society TMS 602-16 Specification for Masonry Structures. Section 3.3 "Unit Masonry" includes: 3.3.1 "Masonry shall be laid in running bond unless otherwise indicated." 3.3.2 "Head joints in adjacent courses shall be offset a minimum of 2 in. (51 mm) or one-fourth the unit length, whichever is greater." Something like that. Also "Bond pattern shall be as indicated." For brick, ASTM C652? But reference TMS 402/602. We'll cite TMS 602 Section 3.3 (Running Bond / Head Joint Offset). Good.

Need output exactly one valid JSON object, no prose. Write to /app/answer.json. In this environment, we can provide JSON content. The instruction says write to /app/answer.json. As assistant, we can output JSON. We should not include markdown code fences? The user says do not include prose, Markdown, code fences, or commentary outside JSON. So final answer should be just raw JSON. We need ensure valid JSON. Use double quotes. No trailing commas.

Let's craft JSON with all fields. decision fail. risk high? Let's decide. Bond pattern broken in block/brick laying can affect structural integrity and is non-compliant. For commercial project, fail, risk high. But maybe medium because not imminent collapse. The schema risk options low/medium/high/critical. Non-compliant structural masonry bond pattern: high. We'll use high.

work_stage rough-complete. percent_complete 85. component_conditions ["installed-defective","non-compliant"]. value 0 (overlap). sound_source "" or null? Schema string. Use "". event_time 0.0, rate 0.0, order [], workable []. findings list. acti…
